data.title: IG Port (TSE:3791) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Growth Narratives
data.description:
IG Port (TSE:3791) has just posted Q2 2026 results with revenue of ¥3,147 million and basic EPS of ¥2.87, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of ¥13,119 million and EPS of ¥31.27 that sit against a reported net profit margin of 4.7%. Over the past six quarters, revenue has moved between ¥3,063 million and ¥4,479 million, while quarterly EPS has ranged from ¥2.87 to ¥12.65, giving investors a clear view of how earnings support has shifted around that margin. With earnings having grown at about 22.2% per year over five years but a weaker trailing margin and earnings outcome, this set of results puts the focus squarely on how durable the current profitability profile really is.

See our full analysis for IG Port.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed growth focused narratives around IG Port and where those stories might need adjusting.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Margins Slide From 7.6% To 4.7%

  • The trailing net profit margin sits at 4.7%, compared with 7.6% a year earlier, alongside trailing net income of ¥612 million on ¥13,119 million of revenue.
  • What stands out for a bullish view that focuses on long term growth is that a 22.2% five year annual earnings growth rate and forecast 36.2% annual earnings growth sit next to this margin step down, so:
    • Supporters of the bullish case can point to revenue forecasted to grow 14.8% a year versus 4.7% for the broader JP market, while acknowledging that profitability per yen of sales has been tighter over the last year.
    • At the same time, critics of that bullish stance can highlight that negative earnings growth in the most recent trailing year and the fall from a 7.6% to 4.7% margin mean delivery against those higher forecasts has become more dependent on future execution than on recent history.
To see how these mixed margin signals fit into the broader growth story, read the full narrative that investors are debating right now. 📊 Read the full IG Port Consensus Narrative.

TTM EPS Slides From ¥56.66 To ¥31.27

  • Trailing twelve month basic EPS is ¥31.27, down from ¥56.66 a year earlier, even though trailing revenue over the same window is still above ¥13.0b.
  • Bears who focus on earnings stability will say this roughly 45% drop in trailing EPS and the range of quarterly EPS from ¥2.87 to ¥12.65 backs their concern about volatility, yet:
    • The presence of five year earnings growth running at 22.2% a year counters the idea that weaker trailing EPS alone defines the business, because it shows that over a longer stretch the company has built up earnings from a lower base.
    • Forecasts for earnings to grow at 36.2% a year add another wrinkle for that bearish view, since if that growth occurs off the current ¥31.27 trailing EPS, the longer term earnings level implied is much higher than where the recent dip might suggest on its own.

High 45.8x P/E Versus 59% DCF Discount

  • The shares trade on a 45.8x P/E against a current price of ¥1,388, while the supplied DCF fair value of ¥3,408.31 implies the stock sits about 59.3% below that modelled value.
  • Supporters of a bullish stance tend to lean on that gap between the P/E and DCF signals as a sign the market is cautious, because:
    • The elevated 45.8x P/E compared with 42.6x for peers and 19.2x for the JP Entertainment industry suggests investors already pay a premium, yet the 59.3% discount to DCF fair value implies the current premium multiple is still well below what that model sees as justified.
    • When that valuation picture is set alongside forecast revenue growth of 14.8% a year and earnings growth of 36.2% a year versus the broader JP market, the bullish argument is that the stock may be priced like a high expectation name, but not yet like one that fully reflects those growth forecasts.

data.title: Shift (TSE:3697) Q1 EPS Softness Tests Bullish Growth And High P/E Narratives
data.description:
SHIFT (TSE:3697) opened FY2026 with Q1 revenue of ¥34.8b and basic EPS of ¥6.82, setting the tone for how its recent growth story is feeding through to the income statement. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from ¥30.8b in Q1 2025 to ¥34.8b in Q1 2026. Over the same quarters, basic EPS went from ¥8.53 to ¥6.82, against a trailing twelve month EPS of ¥33.26 on revenue of ¥134.5b. Taken together with trailing net income of ¥8.75b, the latest results point to a business where earnings power and margins are front of mind for investors trying to judge the consistency of performance.

See our full analysis for SHIFT.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed narratives around SHIFT, highlighting where the story is confirmed and where the figures raise new questions.

Margins Drift as Profit Trails Revenue

  • Net income for Q1 2026 was ¥1,795m on revenue of ¥34,845m, compared with ¥2,653m on ¥34,407m in Q4 2025, so profit did not track the small step up in sales.
  • What stands out to bullish investors is that trailing 12 month net profit margin is 6.5% versus 5.2% the prior year, yet this latest quarter shows profit of ¥1,795m sitting below the recent Q4 figure of ¥2,653m, which
    • supports the bullish argument that the wider 12 month picture still reflects healthier profitability, even if a single quarter comes in softer on the bottom line,
    • but also reminds you that quarterly swings around that 6.5% margin level can still appear when comparing periods like Q1 2026 to Q4 2025.

Earnings Growth Track Record Adds Context

  • Over the last 12 months, earnings grew 45.7% year over year and five year earnings growth averages 23.4% per year. This frames Q1 2026 EPS of ¥6.82 within a much stronger longer term trend.
  • Supporters of the bullish view lean heavily on that 45.7% trailing earnings growth and 23.4% five year average. When you line that up with trailing 12 month EPS of ¥33.26 and trailing revenue of ¥134,490m you get a picture that
    • backs the idea of a business that has produced higher profit over time relative to its earlier base, even though individual quarters like Q1 2026 and Q3 2025 both sit around the ¥6.8 EPS level,

data.title: Iluka Resources Limited's (ASX:ILU) Price Is Right But Growth Is Lacking After Shares Rocket 26%
data.description:
Those holding Iluka Resources Limited (ASX:ILU) shares would be relieved that the share price has rebounded 26% in the last thirty days, but it needs to keep going to repair the recent damage it has caused to investor portfolios. The last 30 days bring the annual gain to a very sharp 28%.

Even after such a large jump in price, Iluka Resources may still be sending bullish signals at the moment with its price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 15.4x, since almost half of all companies in Australia have P/E ratios greater than 22x and even P/E's higher than 41x are not unusual. However, the P/E might be low for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

While the market has experienced earnings growth lately, Iluka Resources' earnings have gone into reverse gear, which is not great. The P/E is probably low because investors think this poor earnings performance isn't going to get any better. If this is the case, then existing shareholders will probably struggle to get excited about the future direction of the share price.

See our latest analysis for Iluka Resources

Want the full picture on analyst estimates for the company? Then our free report on Iluka Resources will help you uncover what's on the horizon.
Advertisement

Is There Any Growth For Iluka Resources?

The only time you'd be truly comfortable seeing a P/E as low as Iluka Resources' is when the company's growth is on track to lag the market.

Taking a look back first, the company's earnings per share growth last year wasn't something to get excited about as it posted a disappointing decline of 30%. This means it has also seen a slide in earnings over the longer-term as EPS is down 62% in total over the last three years. So unfortunately, we have to acknowledge that the company has not done a great job of growing earnings over that time.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the seven analysts covering the company suggest earnings growth is heading into negative territory, declining 6.8% each year over the next three years. Meanwhile, the broader market is forecast to expand by 17% per annum, which paints a poor picture.

With this information, we are not surprised that Iluka Resources is trading at a P/E lower than the market. However, shrinking earnings are unlikely to lead to a stable P/E over the longer term. There's potential for the P/E to fall to even lower levels if the company doesn't improve its profitability.

The Final Word

Iluka Resources' stock might have been given a solid boost, but its P/E certainly hasn't reached any great heights. Generally, our preference is to limit the use of the price-to-earnings ratio to establishing what the market thinks about the overall health of a company.

As we suspected, our examination of Iluka Resources' analyst forecasts revealed that its outlook for shrinking earnings is contributing to its low P/E. Right now shareholders are accepting the low P/E as they concede future earnings probably won't provide any pleasant surprises. It's hard to see the share price rising strongly in the near future under these circumstances.

J. Front Retailing (TSE:3086) reported a 7.7% year-on-year change in consolidated revenue for December 2025, a monthly update that gives investors fresh data on how the department store and related businesses are performing.

See our latest analysis for J. Front Retailing.

The December revenue update comes as J. Front Retailing’s share price sits at ¥2,292.5, with a 7 day share price return of 2.53% and a 30 day share price return of 3.97%. Its 1 year total shareholder return of 18.35% and 5 year total shareholder return of 203.06% point to momentum that has been building over a longer period.

If this kind of steady progress in retail interests you, it could be a good moment to broaden your search with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With December revenue up 7.7% year on year and the share price already reflecting strong 1 and 5 year returns, the key question now is whether J. Front Retailing is still attractively priced or if the market is already assuming future growth.

On a P/E of 19.6x and a last close of ¥2,292.5, J. Front Retailing is priced above both peer and industry averages, which points to a richer valuation rather than a discount story.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price with earnings per share, so a higher multiple usually means the market is paying more for each unit of profit. For a department store group with established operations across retail, real estate related activities and payment services, it is a quick way to see how its earnings are being valued against similar listed companies.

Here, the company trades at 19.6x earnings, compared with a peer average of 18x and a JP Multiline Retail industry average of 16.9x.
